Minimum Average(s) Required.note: See major for required major average.

exceptions: Students admitted with the equivalent of one year of advanced standing must complete the degree requirements within seven calendar years of entry into the plan.
Students admitted with the equivalent of two years of advanced standing must complete the degree requirements within six calendar years of entry into the plan.
Students admitted with one year or more of advanced standing must complete the degree requirements within 10 years of entry into the degree.

Undergraduate Communication Requirement.description: Students are required to complete one course to meet the Undergraduate Communication Requirement.
notes: Students enrolled in the Faculty of Health before September 2017 should consult the English Language Proficiency Requirement described in the Calendar of their entry year.
Successful completion of the Undergraduate Communication Requirement will be recorded on a student's transcript as completion of the Undergraduate Communication Requirement.
Students transferring to another faculty should note that additional courses may be required to satisfy the other faculty's Undergraduate Communication Requirement.

Students transferring into the Faculty of Health who have completed their Undergraduate Communication Requirement in another faculty at the University of Waterloo will be eligible to have it count for their Faculty of Health Undergraduate Communication Requirement.

The Faculty of Health does not permit external transfer credits for HEALTH107 from students transferring from another institution. HEALTH107 must be taken at the University of Waterloo to satisfy the Faculty of Health's Undergraduate Communication Requirement.

Co-operative Education Program Requireme.description: For Bachelor of Arts (Health) students in honours co-op academic plans.

required_courses.PD1: Must be taken in an academic term prior to the first work term.
required_courses.PD12: Must be taken during the first work term.
required_courses.additional: Two additional PD courses to be taken during the second, third, or fourth work terms.

Additional Constraints and Notes: Students must be in an honours academic plan to be eligible for the co-operative (co-op) system of study.
The co-op program is only open to full-time students.
The co-op program is not open to students seeking to complete a second degree.

Students will be removed from their co-op program in the following situations: failure to successfully meet academic program requirements, including minimum averages; or two unemployed or failed work-term opportunities.
Normally, students who are removed from co-op will not be re-admitted into the co-op program.

legend.off: Neither an academic term nor a work term
